name: Integras Capital Recovery
description_short: Specialty holding company in San Antonio that purchases and services distressed financial assets including consumer, real estate, and judgment portfolios across Texas.
description_long:
Integras Capital Recovery LLC is a specialty holding company headquartered in San Antonio, Texas, certified by RMA Receivables Management Association International since April 2015. The company operates in the secondary receivables market, acquiring distressed financial assets that have defaulted with original lenders. Integras purchases the rights to payment on delinquent accounts and then manages collection and servicing of those accounts directly with consumers.

The company specializes in acquiring and servicing distressed portfolios across multiple asset classes including consumer debt, real estate accounts, crowd lending obligations, medical debt, and post-judgment accounts, with operations currently focused on Texas. Their Managed Services Group provides litigation support, collection strategy development, and managed receivership services. According to their mission statement, Integras positions itself around respectful treatment and flexible repayment options designed to fit individual consumer budgets.

Integras differentiates itself through RMA International membership and certification, positioning itself as an industry-regulated debt buyer rather than a traditional debt settlement or consolidation company. The company emphasizes direct account servicing and management rather than third-party negotiation. Their stated commitment to "respectful and honest treatment" and working directly with consumers on customized repayment plans sets them apart from purely litigation-focused debt buying operations.

As a debt buyer, Integras functions differently from consumer-facing debt relief companies. While they claim a consumer-friendly approach, the reality is that purchasing defaulted debt and managing collections inherently involves creditor-side operations. Consumers dealing with Integras would be contacted by a company that now owns their debt obligation, requiring negotiation directly with the debt buyer rather than their original creditor. Verify all communications and account details independently.

When evaluating debt relief companies, consumers should compare settlement programs against alternatives like debt consolidation loans, which combine multiple debts into a single fixed-rate payment. Credit counseling through nonprofit agencies offers free budgeting help without impacting credit scores. For those whose credit has already been damaged, credit repair services can address inaccurate negative items on reports. Personal loans for bad credit may provide funds for debt payoff at lower rates than credit cards, and credit monitoring services help track progress throughout the recovery process. Consolidating high-interest balances into a single installment loan with a fixed rate can reduce total interest paid and simplify monthly budgeting.
address: 3201 Cherry Ridge Dr, San Antonio, TX 78230

company_info.headquarters: 3201 Cherry Ridge Dr, San Antonio, TX 78230

pros: RMA Receivables Management Association International certified since 2015, providing industry oversight and standards compliance
States commitment to flexible, budget-friendly repayment options tailored to individual consumer circumstances
Offers multiple management services including litigation and receivership management beyond basic collections
Texas-based with specialized focus on regional portfolios (consumer, real estate, medical, judgment debt)
Published mission statement emphasizing respectful treatment and willingness to work with consumers on repayment
Designated compliance officer (Anthony Guajardo) on staff for regulatory adherence
Manages diverse portfolio types rather than specializing in single debt category
cons: As a debt buyer, Integras owns defaulted debt and profits from collection—this is a creditor-side operation, not consumer advocacy
Limited public information about actual consumer outcomes, success rates, or dispute resolution processes
No mention of accreditation beyond RMA membership; no BBB rating, consumer reviews, or third-party verification available on website
Website lacks transparency about fees, interest rates, or terms associated with repayment arrangements
No published information about complaint processes, consumer rights protections, or FDCPA compliance procedures
best_for: Texas residents with defaulted accounts that Integras has already purchased who need direct debt servicing
Consumers seeking flexible payment arrangements on existing judgment or post-judgment debt obligations
Individuals with real estate, medical, or crowd lending portfolio debt requiring specialized management
diagnosis: Integras Capital Recovery is suited for Texas consumers who have defaulted on accounts that the company has already purchased and who need to arrange payment directly with the debt buyer. The critical caveat is that Integras operates as a creditor (debt buyer), not as a consumer-side debt relief service—consumers working with them are negotiating with an entity that purchased their debt obligation, not an intermediary working on their behalf.
services: Acquisition of distressed consumer debt portfolios
Acquisition of distressed real estate debt portfolios
Acquisition of crowd lending debt portfolios
Consumer debt collection and payment arrangement negotiation
Account portfolio management for Texas-based distressed assets
